description_long: This object is a detailed stone crucifixion scene, featuring a central Latin cross with a sculpted figure of Jesus Christ affixed to it. Flanking the cross are four life-sized statues, two on each side, representing mourners or saints, all positioned on a rectangular stone platform. The figures are intricately carved, with visible drapery and expressive postures, and the entire ensemble shows signs of weathering and age, with darkened and mossy textures. The base is broad and flat, supporting the weight of the statues and cross, and appears to be made from a single slab of stone or concrete. The rear view reveals the backs of the statues and the cross, with no visible inscriptions or engravings. This monument is typical of graveyard memorials, serving as a focal point for remembrance and reflection.
description: A weathered stone crucifixion scene with a central cross and four mourning statues on a rectangular platform, showing detailed carvings and aged textures.
description_view_0: Front view showing the crucifix with Jesus and four mourning statues, all details and facial expressions visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the cross and statues, with minimal detail visible except the depth of the platform.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the statues and cross, with no visible inscriptions or facial features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the cross and statues, and the length of the platform.
